directions_tried.name: Classical eigenvalue concavity theorems (Davis-Lewis, Ando, etc.)
directions_tried.outcome: Domain gap: all classical theorems operate on vector spaces, not compact groups. λ_max is convex (wrong direction). Best leads: majorization, second-variation, 2024 unitary paper.

directions_tried.name: Geodesic convexity and alternatives on compact Lie groups
directions_tried.outcome: Full concavity impossible by theorem. Six alternative frameworks identified. Top: Morse perfectness (b_{3E}=1), trap-free landscape (Rabitz transversality).

directions_tried.name: Synthesis — proof strategy ranking and critical path
directions_tried.outcome: Matrix domination H(I) >= H(Q) (PSD) emerged as novel top approach. Ranked 8 strategies. Critical path: domination test → landscape survey → Rabitz → Morse.

explorations_completed.goal_summary: Survey classical eigenvalue concavity/convexity theorems and assess applicability to functions on SU(N)^E
explorations_completed.key_finding: All classical theorems have a fundamental domain gap — they operate on vector spaces, not compact groups

explorations_completed.goal_summary: Survey geodesic convexity on Riemannian manifolds and alternatives for certifying global optimality on compact Lie groups
explorations_completed.key_finding: Full geodesic concavity is impossible by theorem on compact manifolds. Morse perfectness and trap-free landscape are the two most promising frameworks.

explorations_completed.goal_summary: Synthesize findings from explorations 001 and 002 into a ranked assessment of proof strategies with critical path
explorations_completed.key_finding: Matrix domination H(I) >= H(Q) (PSD) emerged as novel highest-priority approach, subsumes majorization, directly testable.
